title: Post-merge #93: switch nightly to evolve audit + drain (atomic with marketplace update)
description: After PR #93 (L3 evolve sub-verb namespace) merges, do these in ONE sitting so the 03:17 nightly never no-ops: (1) advance local main to origin/main; (2) claude plugin marketplace update vdsmon-flow; (3) edit ~/.flow-evolve/nightly-evolve.sh line ~70 'claude --bg /flow evolve' -> 'evolve audit' and line ~81 'claude --bg /flow evolve --ship' -> 'evolve drain'. Then live-smoke the dispatch (the only L3 verification gates cannot do): /flow evolve -> sub-verb listing; /flow evolve launch --dry-run -> plan no action; /flow evolve drain --dry-run -> both plans no action. Hazard if non-atomic: nightly fires bare evolve (now lists, files nothing) + evolve --ship (now unknown sub-verb) = silent no-op night.
close_reason: Done: #93 merged, local main advanced to d5233b7, marketplace updated, nightly-evolve.sh switched to 'evolve audit' + 'evolve drain' (syntax-checked), dispatch live-smoked (bare evolve -> listing; launch/janitor backends produce plans).

title: Jira adapter write/mutation methods have zero test coverage (create, link, all setters, board_rank, attachments)
description:
EVIDENCE:
tracker_jira.py defines create (554), set_summary (580), set_description (588), set_priority (591), set_labels (594), set_assignee (597), link (649), board_rank (819), get/upload_attachments, set_components, set_custom_field. Verified: grep for `.create(`/`.link(`/`.set_summary(` in test_jira_adapter.py = 0; the 42 tests cover only reads/transitions/sprint/watcher/fix-versions/epic-link. The beads equivalents ARE tested (test_beads_adapter.py:333,353,365 call adapter.create). These methods build branching REST payloads (create: plain-vs-ADF summary, optional parent/labels/assignee at 563-577), so a payload-shape regression ships silently to live Jira.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Jira write paths are how /flow mutates real tickets; an untested PUT/POST payload that Jira rejects or mis-applies corrupts tracker state for paying users, and the asymmetry means the cheaper backend (beads) is the verified one. High value, strong evidence — but many methods, so not 'small/isolated'; ranks below the one-liners. Touches only the test file.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tests/test_jira_adapter.py
HOT FILE: False

title: pending-mutations queue is built + tested but no adapter or stage ever enqueues; transient tracker mutations are silently lost
description:
EVIDENCE:
Verified: append_mutation has zero non-test, non-self callers (grep of append_mutation across *.py returns only its definition at pending_mutations.py:152, a docstring, and the __all__ export). Only the drain side is wired: sync.py:87 list_mutations, :101 compact. tracker_beads.py and tracker_jira.py contain zero 'pending' references. The commit-stage transient-failure branch (stage-commit.md:94-97) logs a warning and continues — it drops the mutation, never enqueues. sync.py:3 and verb-sync-baseline.md assert the file is 'written by adapter failure paths' — false for both adapters. This is the unaddressed core of the stale TODO at tracker_beads.py:19-23 ('TODO (phase 8): transient-failure paths should append to pending-mutations.jsonl ... until phase 8 ships'), which contradicts dev-history.md (phase 8d shipped /flow sync) — fold the stale-TODO finding in here, it is the breadcrumb for the same unwired enqueue.

VALUE / RATIONALE:
Highest value of the set: in unattended runs a network/auth blip on a tracker transition (or comment/link/edit) is dropped permanently and /flow sync can never reconcile it because nothing wrote it. The whole pending-mutations subsystem (module + CLI + drain + verb prose) is dead weight until a write path is wired in. BUT: blast-radius-safety pulls this down — wiring append_mutation into the commit/ticket transient-failure branches touches wired adapters (tracker_beads/jira) and stage-commit.md, so it is NOT a safe small ship. Rank high on value, flag the tradeoff. Touches wired handlers = hot.

BLAST RADIUS: plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_beads.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/tracker_jira.py, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-commit.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/references/stage-ticket.md, plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/pending_mutations.py

title: ticket stage: claim tracker backend (open->in_progress) to prevent fleet double-pick
description: The ticket stage (references/stage-ticket.md, handler=inline) stamps .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md frontmatter status=in_progress but never transitions the tracker BACKEND. On beads the bd issue stays 'open' for the whole run, so 'bd ready' still lists the in-flight ticket as available and a parallel fleet agent could double-pick it. Fix: add a best-effort backend transition (open->in_progress) to the ticket stage, mirroring the stage-commit transition step (MCP-first, REST/tracker_cli fallback), tolerant of exit 3 (no transition available / already in_progress) and transient faults so it never fails the run. Surfaced on flow-7qm with the machinery lens ON (PROPOSE+RECORD).

title: concurrent /flow <key> --auto launches both bootstrap a worktree for the same bead; worktree-local lease cannot serialize them
description: EVIDENCE (flow-wsv, 2026-06-06): two /flow flow-wsv --auto runs launched ~8s apart both ran to a PR. PR #123 (branch feature/flow-wsv-audit-drift-all-loci, created 08:48:45) and PR #124 (branch feature/flow-wsv-evolve-audit-enumerate-all-loci, created 08:55:22) carried the SAME three-file fix for the SAME bead; #123 self-merged at 08:53:58 and closed the bead, #124 was superseded (CLOSED, never merged) and its worktree reaped out from under the running session (cwd-deleted mid-stage). ROOT CAUSE: the run lease is <ticket_dir>/run.lock with ticket_dir=.flow/runs/<key>/ living INSIDE each run's own worktree (lease.py:135 run_lock_path, :13 docstring; flow_worktree.py create seeds a fresh worktree+.flow per run). Two bootstraps of the same bead create two independent ticket_dirs -> two independent run.lock files; the flock at lease.py:223 only serializes acquire/refresh/release WITHIN one worktree, never across worktrees, so dispatch_stage.py init returns resumed:true for each in isolation and neither sees the sibling. The bead-status signal that COULD catch it is discarded: #123's run had already moved the bead open->in_progress, so #124's ticket-stage claim hit exit 3 'no transition to in_progress available', but stage-ticket.md step 3b reads exit 3 as the benign self-resume case and continues silently, so a sibling's claim is indistinguishable from one's own resume. The drain loop's evolve_select drops in-flight beads, but a direct /flow <key> --auto launch bypasses select entirely, so the lease is the only intended guard and it does not span worktrees. VALUE: wasted a full unattended run (worktree, commit, PR, CI) and risks a confusing mid-stage cwd-deletion when the loser is reaped; the duplicate version-bump only auto-resolved because the deterministic patch bump made both PRs byte-identical (flow-8we), masking a DIRTY race in the general case. FIX DIRECTION: add a pre-bootstrap duplicate-claim guard at the spec/flow_worktree.py-create choke point (or a canonical shared per-ticket lease under the main .flow, acquired BEFORE worktree creation, keyed by ticket only) that refuses to bootstrap a second concurrent run when the bead is already in_progress with a live sibling run/branch/worktree, distinguishing a true self-resume (same run_id) from a sibling claim. BLAST RADIUS: touches the bootstrap/lease seam (flow_worktree.py, lease.py, possibly verb-spec.md) - guard/safety machinery, so HOT.

title: evolve audit doc-drift miner should enumerate ALL loci of a PR-introduced drift, not just one file
description: MACHINERY (minor): the §audit doc-drift miner (verb-evolve.md:47, a read-only agent fan-out) filed flow-t97 scoped to references/verb-evolve.md only, but PR #119's drain-termination vocabulary drift (liveness-gated/non-live-lease/'no run is live') also lived in references/self-evolution.md lines 37+39 (the drain-loop overview). The flow-t97 --auto run discovered the second locus only at implement-time via a repo-wide grep, forcing a mid-run planned_files reconcile (RECONCILE friction) to fold it in; had the grep not run, an identical drift would have shipped unfixed for a follow-up that re-triggers a full CI loop. Fix: instruct the doc-drift miner (verb-evolve.md §audit, line ~47) that PR-introduced vocabulary drift typically spans every reference file describing the same subsystem, so a drift finding must grep the whole references/ + SKILL.md doc set for the stale phrasing and enumerate ALL loci (every file path) in one bead's planned-files, rather than scoping to the single file the diff happened to surface.

title: ci-rollup classifies transient CANCELLED (superseded duplicate run) as CI failure
description: forge_github._classify_rollup (plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/forge_github.py:184-227) maps any terminal non-SUCCESS verdict (CANCELLED/NEUTRAL/SKIPPED/STALE) to status='failed', and any_failed wins over any_pending. When a PR has duplicate concurrent workflow runs (push + pull_request on the same SHA), GitHub's concurrency group cancels the superseded run; the transient CANCELLED conclusion makes ci-rollup return 'failed' for a moment though every real check ends SUCCESS. Surfaced in flow-483 PR #120: review_loop CI Monitor broke on a spurious 'failed' (gh pr checks all-pass, re-poll green). Unattended this trips a phantom fix cycle / STATUS=failed on a green PR. Fix: treat CANCELLED as pending (re-run/superseded), not failed; consider NEUTRAL/SKIPPED too. Do NOT fold CANCELLED into green — the green gate must stay 'every check completed-SUCCESS' to match evolve_reap.rollup_is_green (hot auto-merge gate). Knowledge entry id 87ff08752652e2c1.

title: verb-evolve.md drain-termination prose omits corrupt-blocking (PR #119 drift)
description: MACHINERY (minor): flow-wo5/PR #119 made evolve_drain.decide treat a 'corrupt' lease as BLOCKING (wait on live OR corrupt; done requires neither) and updated evolve_drain.py's module docstring, but references/verb-evolve.md §drain 'The loop' (lines ~129-133) still describes wait as 'a still-LIVE in-flight run', done as 'no in-flight run is live', and 'termination is liveness-gated', omitting that a corrupt lease now also blocks until a human runs recover takeover. Doc drift introduced by the PR; verb-evolve.md was not in planned_files so it could not ride along without tripping content-ownership. Fix: update those three loop bullets + the 'liveness-gated on purpose' sentence to say a run blocks when its lease is live OR corrupt.

title: state.read creates state.json.lock before checking state.json existence
description: MACHINERY (minor, hot): state.read (plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts/state.py:195) enters 'with flock_blocking(_lock_path(td))' BEFORE checking state.json existence. flock_blocking creates state.json.lock and its parent dirs, so a read against a missing/wrong ticket-dir materializes <td>/.flow/runs/<key>/state.json.lock instead of cleanly reporting absence. Surfaced in flow-wo5 when a dispatch advance ran with --workspace-root resolving to the scripts dir (shell cwd drift across Bash calls); the stray scripts/.flow/runs/flow-wo5/state.json.lock then tripped commit check-ownership exit 3, needing a manual rm + re-check. Fix candidate: stat the state.json path before acquiring the flock in state.read (state.py:189-200) and return the not-found tuple early, so a read never leaves lock/dir residue for a non-existent run. state.py is named safety machinery -> property-check review.

title: nested .flow/ from a wrong-cwd dispatch leaks run-state into the commit ownership gate
description: MACHINERY (minor): the do-loop dispatch calls use --workspace-root . and assume cwd == worktree root, but nothing enforces it. If a prior bash step leaves cwd in a subdir (e.g. plugins/flow/skills/flow/scripts after a 'cd scripts && pytest'), a dispatch_stage advance run there either errors ('no state.json at .../scripts/.flow/runs/<KEY>') or, on init, creates a phantom scripts/.flow/runs/<KEY>/ tree. Root .gitignore:28-38 anchors its .flow patterns to repo root ('.flow/runs/' contains a mid-pattern slash, so git anchors it), so a nested .flow/ is NOT ignored. The 0-byte scripts/.flow/runs/flow-ghd/state.json.lock then surfaced as an unowned change and tripped diff_extract.py check-ownership (exit 3) at the commit stage. The gate did its job (refused, never silently committed), so severity is minor. Fix candidates: (a) add an unanchored '**/.flow/' rule (or per-pattern '**/.flow/runs/' etc.) to .gitignore so a nested .flow can't leak; (b) harden the do-loop prose so dispatch bash steps cd to the worktree root first, or have dispatch_stage refuse to init/advance when cwd is not the resolved workspace root. EVIDENCE: .gitignore:28 ('.flow/runs/' root-anchored), diff_extract.py check-ownership exit 3 path. NOTE: .gitignore is a repo/PR artifact so this was NOT applied at reflect-time (post-PR-churn boundary).

title: planned_files omits version-bump files -> reconcile on every plugin-code evolve bead
description: Every evolve bead that edits flow PLUGIN code pays a post-implement reconcile: planned_files is stamped at bootstrap from the plan's 'Files to change' and never includes the two version-bump files (plugins/flow/.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), yet the implement stage ALWAYS bumps them per the version-bump invariant (stage-implement.md:45). The implement subagent flags them and the documented reconcile (widen planned_files -> re-record baseline -> re-capture diff) handles it cleanly, but it costs a round-trip on every plugin-code ticket. Fix candidate: have spec/bootstrap auto-include the two version-bump files in planned_files whenever the change touches the plugin (flow self-target), OR have stage-plan.md instruct planners to list them under 'Files to change'. Evidence: flow-5dh run hit RECONCILE friction; stage-implement.md:45 (version bump), verb-spec.md step 6 (planned_files stamping). Linked knowledge entry id d0c98340c59bdc1d.

title: BeadsAdapter.get() returns zero comments live: omits --include-comments and reads body not text
description: EVIDENCE: tracker_beads.py get() calls _run_json(['show', key]) WITHOUT --include-comments, and _comment_from_json reads raw.get('body') while bd keys comment bodies under 'text'. Against the live DB this makes adapter.get(key)['comments'] ALWAYS empty (verified: adapter.get('flow-2pp')->0 comments, raw _run_json(['show','flow-2pp','--include-comments'])->2). IMPACT: /flow triage list shows '(no open-question comment)' for every real deferred bead (the _open_question scan sees no comments); any get()-based comment consumer is silently blind. flow-6rl's decided() probe and its triage-list blocked-surfacing both sidestep this by doing their own raw 'show --include-comments' fetch reading 'text', so decided-mode is unaffected; this bead is the underlying adapter fix. FIX: add --include-comments to get()'s show call and make _comment_from_json read raw.get('text') (fall back to 'body'). BLAST RADIUS: adapter.get() is called broadly; adding --include-comments means every get() now fetches comments (perf + a behavior change for callers that did not expect them). Judgment call -> propose, not auto-apply. Owning: scripts/tracker_beads.py get + _comment_from_json. NOT hot (core adapter, not a guard file).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: ticket flags this 'propose, not auto-apply' (blast radius: get() is called broadly, e.g. triage.py, tracker_cli.py, so every caller would start fetching comments). OPEN DECISION: make get() fetch comments unconditionally (matches the FIX text; simplest) vs gate behind an opt-in param (get(key, *, include_comments=False)) so only triage opts in and existing callers' perf/behavior stays untouched. Mechanical fix is otherwise fully specified and verified: (1) get() show call -> add --include-comments; (2) _comment_from_json read raw.get('text') then fall back to 'body'. TDD tests: assert --include-comments in show args; assert a text-keyed comment marshals into body; assert body-keyed fallback still works (keeps existing fixture green). To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: evolve_select blind to pre-PR live runs: premature drain 'done' + unenforced hot serialization
description:
EVIDENCE: evolve_select.py:188 _gather_refs joins in-flight via 'gh pr list' + remote 'git for-each-ref' (branch-name join, _is_inflight line 102). A launched run in early stages (plan/implement/code_review, pre-push) has only a LOCAL worktree branch -> no remote ref, no PR -> _is_inflight=False.
CONSEQUENCE 1: evolve_drain returns action=done with liveness {} while a launched run is genuinely live. Observed 2026-06-06: flow-56s live in implement (lease pid 85395 exp 05:52Z), flow-6rl live in code_review (lease pid 85602 exp 05:37Z); select returned done, held_hot=[flow-eel,flow-ghd,flow-wo5,flow-yga]. Obeying 'done' strands the held hot beads until a human re-runs drain.
CONSEQUENCE 2: _hot_inflight (line 211) can't see a pre-PR hot run, so one-hot-at-a-time serialization is unenforced. During this drain flow-56s was launched while flow-6rl was still live (both hot) -> two parallel hot runs, risking a self-merge race under auto_merge_hot.
FIX: detect in-flight from the per-ticket lease/run-state (.flow/runs/<key>/run.lock, live unexpired lease), not only remote refs/PRs. evolve_drain already reads leases for liveness; feed that into select inflight_keys + _hot_inflight so a pre-PR live run counts -> action=wait (not done) while live, and a live hot blocks launching another hot.
VALUE: closes a drain correctness gap (premature termination + double-launch of hot beads). BLAST RADIUS: evolve_select.py partition/select + evolve_drain liveness wiring + tests for pre-PR inflight detection.

title: do-loop self-drift on stage-registry.toml/handlers halts: owned-reconcile carve-out misses stage_registry when skill_root==worktree
description:
ROOT CAUSE: in the spec->EnterWorktree->do flow the do-loop executes scripts from the run's worktree, so _skill_root_from_script() (dispatch_stage.py:129,305; snapshot.py:50,174) resolves skill_root to the WORKTREE, not a separate main checkout. compute_snapshot reads the stage_registry component from stage_registry_path(skill_root) = worktree/stage-registry.toml (snapshot.py:174).

CONSEQUENCE A (this run, flow-alz): a ticket whose deliverable IS editing stage-registry.toml drifts the snapshot's stage_registry component (classify_drift snapshot.py:208). The owned-drift auto-reconcile only covers workspace_toml-sole drift (flow-u3s / PR#66), so the next dispatch_stage.py advance returns exit 1 'config/version drift mid-run' and forces a manual recover.py reload-snapshot detour. An unattended --auto run following SKILL.md's literal drift handling ('surface + /flow recover, break the loop') would HALT and defer instead of completing. The same applies to any wired skill: handler edited in-worktree.

CONSEQUENCE B: self-evolution.md step 2b claims machinery APPLY-NOW edits carry zero re-review churn because the skill checkout is a separate tree from the run's worktree. In this self-target dogfood flow skill_root == worktree, so a machinery edit to the worktree skill tree lands in the OPEN PR and re-triggers CI + review. The churn-free premise is false here.

FIX PROPOSAL (maintainer judgment, not auto-applied): extend the owned-drift carve-out so a drift whose changed component files all map to the run's planned_files (a deliberate, self-inflicted edit) auto-reloads the snapshot and continues, while still halting on foreign/concurrent drift; and correct/condition the self-evolution.md churn-free claim for the skill_root==worktree case. Touches snapshot.py (classify_drift), dispatch_stage.py (advance drift path), SKILL.md do-loop drift handling, references/verb-do.md, references/self-evolution.md.

EVIDENCE: snapshot.py:13-14,174,208; dispatch_stage.py:129,305,233; remediated this run via recover.py reload-snapshot (recover verb). Friction id 7ccf715c (DRIFT/major).

title: seam_check.py does not guard SKILL.md/prose <-> stage-registry.toml mapping drift
description: flow-0n8 removed a '## Stage handler routing' section in SKILL.md that statically re-enumerated the stage->reference_doc mapping already canonical in stage-registry.toml (reference_doc fields). Root cause it was able to rot: seam_check.py (scripts/seam_check.py) only validates prose script-invocation seams (${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py calls), it has NO check that prose listings of stage->reference_doc stay in sync with stage-registry.toml. The registry header (stage-registry.toml:2-3) says adding a stage = 'append entry + drop a reference doc' with no SKILL.md update step, so any re-introduced enumeration silently drifts. FIX: add a seam_check pass (mirroring the existing scripts_missing_from_module_md helper, flow-aut.9) that flags any SKILL.md/reference prose that re-lists stage->reference_doc mappings, or asserts none exist outside the registry. Hot file (SKILL.md + stage-registry.toml + seam_check.py).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: the fix design is sound (a count-based seam_check guard, mirroring scripts_missing_from_module_md, flagging any doc that cites > N distinct registry stage->reference_doc mappings — robust where structural/pairing heuristics are not), but one MAINTAINER-WEIGHABLE design knob is unresolved: the threshold N. Live corpus max distinct stage-doc cites per doc = 2 (SKILL.md and verb-evolve.md). N=3 (max+1) is a tight tripwire but margin-of-1: any future doc legitimately citing a 3rd stage-doc trips it. N=5 (majority-of-registry) gives headroom but misses a partial 3-4 re-list. The ticket's stated alternative ('asserts none exist outside the registry') is infeasible as-worded since legit single/double cites exist (max 2). Pick the policy: tight tripwire (N=3) vs headroom (N=5). Two concrete corrections found during planning, fold them into the implementation when reopened: (1) the citation/registry regex MUST use char class [a-z0-9_]+ (NOT [a-z_]+) or it silently misses stage-e2e.md (the digit) — registry parse would return 9 docs not 10. (2) DROP the planned stage-registry.toml header-comment edit: it violates the 'never self-edit stage-registry.toml mid-run' invariant (and the snapshot-drift it causes in spec->EnterWorktree->do, flow-alz/PR#111); the fix is seam_check.py + tests only, no registry/SKILL.md edit needed. To unstick: answer the threshold here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: dispatch_stage.py module docstring omits the advance and release subcommands
description: EVIDENCE: dispatch_stage.py:5 docstring states 'Subcommands: init, next, finish, status' but the actual CLI (argparse subparsers, dispatch_stage.py:483-504) registers SIX: init, next, advance, finish, release, status. advance and release are missing from the docstring. MODULE.md:11 already lists all six correctly, so the script's own header is the stale surface. VALUE: corrects the module's self-documentation (advance = finish+next round-trip and release are both on the do-loop hot path, SKILL.md steps 4e/5). BLAST RADIUS: docstring-only edit to dispatch_stage.py; no code/behavior/guard-property change (provably-safe). FIX: update line 5 to list init, next, advance, finish, release, status.

title: seam_check.py does not lint /flow <verb> --flag prose against each verb's argparse surface
description: EVIDENCE: seam_check.py:34 _SCRIPT_RE = r'\$\{CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR\}/scripts/([a-z_]+\.py)' only matches python3 scripts/<x>.py invocations in prose; it never cross-checks slash-prose like '/flow <verb> --flag' in references/*.md against the argparse surface of scripts/<verb>.py. So a reference doc that names a phantom or renamed verb flag passes the seam gate. CORROBORATION (git history of reactive doc-fix PRs that a prose-flag lint would have gated at merge time): #103 'redirect phantom flags', #95 'reconcile prose shows...', plus #90/#92/#77/#82 doc reconciles. The reflect/knowledge store carries this as MACHINERY-major (flow-bbb): proposed as a prose-lint pass, never landed; still present in code. VALUE: converts a recurring reactive doc-drift class into a pre-merge gate failure; strengthens the prose<->CLI seam invariant that is core to the thesis. BLAST RADIUS: extends seam_check.py (not a guard file, not hot); additive lint, the risk is false positives, mitigated by scoping to documented verbs. FIX: add a pass that, for each '/flow <verb> --flag' (and bare-token verbs) in SKILL.md + references/*.md, resolves the verb to its scripts/<verb>.py (or the verb's documented flag set) and flags any --flag the argparse surface lacks; gate it under tests/test_seam_check.py like the existing live-docs check.
close_reason: duplicate of flow-eel (same seam_check verb-flag prose-lint gap)

title: stage-registry.toml header doc drift: required_fields undocumented; roles overclaims recognised reflect_anchor/ship_observer
description: EVIDENCE: (1) The registry field-semantics header (stage-registry.toml:9-24) documents name/default_handler/roles/etc. but OMITS required_fields entirely, yet required_fields is live and load-bearing: used at lines 69 (implement: planned_files), 98 (e2e: e2e_recipe), 113 (commit: commit_type, commit_summary) and consumed by _registry.py:30,69 + lint_ticket.py:47-55,74 to drive ticket-frontmatter linting. title: SKILL.md 'Stage handler routing' section duplicates the registry (thinness + 3rd drift surface)

This is neither router, spec gate, nor do-loop skeleton, so it violates the SKILL.md thinness invariant (CLAUDE.md: 'SKILL.md stays thin'). The mapping is already canonical in stage-registry.toml reference_doc fields (lines 43,57,72,86,101,116,130,144,158,172) and the execution path reads descriptor.reference_doc (SKILL.md:127,129), never this list -> dead duplication. title: Snapshot write failure silently disables drift detection for the whole run (fail-open)
description: EVIDENCE: dispatch_stage.py:183 wraps the canonical-snapshot write in `with contextlib.suppress(Exception): write_snapshot(...)` and cmd_init still returns exit 0. snapshot.py:244-245 classify_drift returns `(True, 'no snapshot to verify', [])` when snapshot.sha is absent. So if the init write raises (ENOSPC/EACCES at snapshot.py:192-195 atomic_write_text, or an OSError from _tree_hash read_bytes during a handler-plugin reinstall/file delete), no snapshot.sha is ever written, init succeeds, and EVERY subsequent next/advance reports drift_ok=True. The TOCTOU drift guard is silently off for the run's entire life and the original error is swallowed (nothing logs it). SCENARIO: a workspace.toml edit, stage-registry swap, or wired-handler code change mid-run goes undetected, defeating exactly the threat snapshot.py exists to stop. VALUE: closes a fail-open hole in load-bearing safety machinery (TIGHTEN-not-erode). BLAST RADIUS: touches snapshot.py + dispatch_stage.py (both guard files) -> HOT, must ride the in-run guard-property review. FIX OPTIONS: either do not suppress the init write (fail init if the canonical snapshot cannot be established), or have classify_drift distinguish 'intentionally absent' from 'expected-but-missing' via a sentinel written on successful init so a missing-but-expected snapshot aborts rather than passes.
close_reason: duplicate of flow-ghd (same snapshot fail-open; flow-ghd carries the fail-closed fix)

title: Triaged+reopened bead re-defers under auto-mode on the already-answered question (triage loop has no exit)
description:
PROBLEM: spec auto-mode (--auto) has one disposition when it cannot self-approve — defer-and-exit (verb-spec.md defer recipe). A bead the maintainer just triaged and reopened (status open, DECISION comment posted by the triage reopen recipe, verb-triage.md) can hit auto-mode again (e.g. via evolve drain) and RE-DEFER on the exact question already answered. The triage->reopen->re-defer cycle never converges. Repro: evolve drain --include-proposals deferred 11 judgment beads; maintainer answered all via triage (comment + transition --to-state open); re-running auto-mode would re-defer them.

ROOT CAUSE: the 90pct confidence gate conflates two uncertainties — (1) JUDGMENT (what to build), resolved by the triage answer; (2) IMPLEMENTATION (can I build it correctly), NOT resolved by triage. A defer on (1) must never recur post-triage; a defer on (2) is legitimate but silently dropping out of bd ready is wrong.

DESIGN — decided mode (maintainer-approved 2026-06-05):
1. MARKER: the triage reopen path (verb-triage.md / triage.py) must stamp a durable, detectable marker on the bead when it reopens-with-answer — a triaged label and/or a stable comment stem (the answers posted use a DECISION: prefix; do not rely on free text — add a structured marker). This is the signal that judgment is settled.
2. DETECTION: spec/auto-mode, at gate time, detects the marker. When present it runs in decided mode automatically (no extra flag needed for the drain path, which launches /flow KEY --auto unchanged). An explicit --decided flag exists as the override / manual form and for clarity.
3. DECIDED BEHAVIOR: (a) ingest the DECISION comment(s) into the planner context as authoritative on the open question, so judgment-uncertainty is gone; (b) FORBID defer. If the planner still cannot reach 90pct on a NEW implementation-level wall: if the bead is HOT (touches a hot/guard file per the verb-evolve step-2 hot-file set) -> BLOCK and surface (failed/recover, parked draft PR, stays visible), NEVER blind-ship; if the bead is CLEAN -> PROCEED best-effort with the strongest plan. NEVER silently re-defer in decided mode.
4. GUARDRAIL: no-defer must never mean blind-ship on hot/guard work. The hot->block floor is load-bearing (flow-2pp drift guard, flow-wo5 lease.py are live examples in the current queue).

FILES (hot — rides the hot path, guard-review gates the merge): references/verb-spec.md (defer recipe + the decided branch), SKILL.md (spec gate prose — keep thin), references/verb-triage.md + scripts/triage.py (stamp the marker on reopen), references/verb-evolve.md (drain auto-picks decided via marker; document), plus tests for the marker stamp + the decided disposition matrix (hot->block, clean->proceed, judgment-answered->no-redefer). Run seam_check.py after prose edits.

VALUE: closes the triage cycle so a human answer actually sticks; unblocks autonomous drain of triaged judgment work without an infinite defer loop. BLAST RADIUS: changes the auto-mode disposition surface; gated behind the triaged marker so untriaged auto-mode behavior is unchanged (still defers). HOT.
acceptance_criteria:
AC1 (critical, anti-self-defeat): detection MUST trigger on the DECISION: comment stem ALREADY present on the 9 reopened beads (flow-0mi flow-dwd flow-2pp flow-837 flow-wo5 flow-g3e flow-148 flow-8q6 flow-vuc), verified against the actual triage.py reopen code — OR the plan includes a post-merge re-stamp of those 9. They must NOT re-defer after this ships (that is the exact bug). triage.py must stamp the same marker reliably going forward.
AC2 (classifier conservatism): the clean-vs-hot split reuses the EXACT verb-evolve step-2 hot/guard file set (do not reinvent it) and DEFAULTS TO HOT/BLOCK when the file set is ambiguous. Rationale: in this maintainer self-target repo, clean->proceed-best-effort = self-merge of a green PR at <90% gated ONLY by CI; CI catches broken, not wrong. The classifier is load-bearing for autonomous merges.
AC3 (YAGNI): drop the explicit --decided flag unless a concrete manual-override caller is named in the plan; marker auto-detection is the sole trigger (covers the drain path + the motivating case). Extra flag = surface on hot files verb-spec.md + SKILL.md gate.
AC4 (expectation): document that HOT triaged beads (flow-2pp, flow-wo5) still BLOCK on any residual wall; only CLEAN triaged beads self-drain autonomously. This feature drains the clean subset, not all 9.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: the plan is solid (88%) but rests on one unsettled JUDGMENT fork the maintainer must decide. At the spec --auto gate (pre-bootstrap, no PR exists yet), how should a HOT + decided + sub-90%-implementation bead behave?
(A) Still bootstrap and proceed; let the EXISTING evolve merge-gate park its draft PR for guard-review (held_guard/skipped_hot, verb-evolve.md §drain) plus a surfacing comment. Matches 'parked draft PR, stays visible'; avoids re-introducing a relaunch loop.
(B) Have the spec gate itself REFUSE to bootstrap and keep the bead visible another way. Matches the word 'BLOCK', but needs an extra no-relaunch marker or it recreates the very loop this ticket closes.
The plan assumes (A). This is a hot disposition-surface change to the never-blind-ship floor, so the fork should be settled by a human, not blind-picked. To unstick: answer here (A or B), reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: seam_check can't lint /flow <verb> --flag slash-prose (let phantom recover flags ship)
description: seam_check.py lints only ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py invocations (its _SCRIPT_RE); /flow <verb> --x slash-prose in references/*.md is uncatchable. This let flow-bbb's 5 phantom /flow recover --reset-*/--reapply-implement flags ship in references/stage-ticket.md + references/stage-commit.md against a recover.py surface of only {detect,takeover,abort,reload-snapshot,retry,skip}. Proposed fix: a prose-lint pass that greps /flow <verb> --x across references and cross-checks each verb's flags against the matching scripts/<verb>.py argparse surface. Owner: scripts/seam_check.py. title: implement stage verifies tests but not the project lint/format gate, so formatter-dirty code fails CI
description: stage-implement.md step 5 (references/stage-implement.md:55-58) instructs only 'Run the project's full relevant test suite ... iterate until green'. It never tells the implement subagent to run the project's lint/format gate that CI enforces. On flow-2ec the subagent ran 'ruff check' (lint) ad hoc but not 'ruff format --check'; the new test file was lint-clean but formatter-dirty, passed implement, then failed the CI 'lint' job ('1 file would be reformatted'), costing a review_loop fix cycle (reformat + commit 9835f58 + push). Fix: stage-implement.md step 5 should require running the project's full CI-equivalent gate before declaring green, explicitly including any formatter --check step (a file can be lint-clean yet format-dirty), not just the linter and tests. For this repo that gate is 'mise run lint' (ruff check + ruff format --check + ty) AND 'mise run test'. Severity: minor (one self-healed cycle, no manual intervention). Owning file: references/stage-implement.md.

title: Tracker Capability.payload_schema + advertised capabilities list is scaffolding for a never-built capability cross-check (strip or build?)
description:
tracker.py:155 declares payload_schema: dict[str, Any] | None on the Capability TypedDict; it is populated as None across all 28 advertisement sites (tracker_jira.py:67-80 and tracker_beads.py:86-99, 14 caps each) and read by ZERO consumers (grep for payload_schema finds only the literal-None entries + the field decl + its comment). The comment at tracker.py:151-154 says a validator rejects extra/edit payloads that exercise a capability where supported=false using this schema, but validate_workspace.py:8-9 explicitly defers the capability cross-check to phase 7-full and no such validator exists. The advertised capabilities list itself (tracker.py:273, forge.py:137) is read only by round-trip tests (tests/test_tracker.py:42-43, test_beads_adapter.py:118); the real per-method gate is the NotSupported raise in each typed op, not a list read. forge.py's own Capability omits payload_schema entirely, confirming the field never matched a real need.

EVIDENCE: tracker.py:155 (field decl), :151-154 (comment promising validator), :273 (capabilities list); tracker_jira.py:67-80, tracker_beads.py:86-99 (28 None sites); validate_workspace.py:8-9 (cross-check deferred to 7-full); forge.py:99,137 (Capability omits payload_schema). Roadmap mention is inventory.md:513 inside the archived build-log table; no open bead, no in-flight branch.

BLAST RADIUS: tracker.py Capability TypedDict + 28 None-valued sites in the two tracker adapters; any future capability cross-check would need either the field rebuilt or the deferred feature shipped. forge Capability unaffected (already omits it). Strip is mechanical but the strip-vs-build choice is judgment.

CONFIDENCE: 0.5

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion -- this is the same declared-but-unconsumed forward-contract template as flow-vuc; the strip-vs-build call is genuinely judgment, and two beads from one pattern suggests a maintainer curation question on whether to mass-file one bead per unconsumed field.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: strip-vs-build judgment call, not mechanical. The strip is trivial (payload_schema: 28 None sites, zero consumers, forge omits the field, validator deferred to 7-full and never built), but the DIRECTION — strip the forward-contract field vs build the deferred capability cross-check (inventory.md:513) — is a maintainer call, and pairs with flow-vuc as a 'one bead per unconsumed field?' curation question. To unstick: decide strip vs build here, reopen (status->open), re-run WITHOUT --auto.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: STRIP the field. Remove Capability.payload_schema (28 None sites, zero consumers, forge omits it, validator never built). Behavior-preserving.

title: time-to-PR is the unfiled third leg of the vision's measurement trio (metric.py exposes only tickets-per-week)
description:
VISION names 'faster time-to-PR' FIRST in the measurement trio ('faster time-to-PR, fewer friction events per run, a lower revert rate -- recall --metric is the seam', VISION.md:54) yet metric.py:357 registers exactly ONE subparser, tickets-per-week. The other two legs are filed (flow-148 revert-rate, flow-eia friction-per-run); time-to-PR has NO bead. Unlike flow-148, the feedstock EXISTS today: state.py:50,52,66 records run started_at + per-stage started_at_iso/finished_at_iso, and observe_ship_event.py:83-85 validates shipped_at in an immutable ticket-keyed ship-event, so both plan-start->create_pr-finish and ticket-fetch->shipped_at durations are computable from already-captured immutable evidence (no new capture seam).

EVIDENCE: metric.py:357 (single tickets-per-week subparser); VISION.md:54 (time-to-PR first in trio); state.py:50,52,66; observe_ship_event.py:83-85; recall.py:232-235 (--metric passthrough already exists).

Read-only over existing JSONL/state, no writer, no schema change. Design choice: which stage pair anchors the duration (plan-start->create_pr-finish vs ticket-fetch->shipped_at).

CONFIDENCE: 0.74

title: _runner.py hosts two runner contracts but a third (cwd-bound closure) is reinvented locally in 7 modules
description:
EVIDENCE: _runner.py exports only Contract A (positional-cwd, default_runner() @ _runner.py:11/14) and Contract B (keyword-only, kw_default_runner() @ _runner.py:22/25); MODULE.md:96 documents only these two. A third shape — Runner = Callable[[list[str]], CompletedProcess[str]] with cwd closed into the closure — has no home, so it is re-declared locally in 7 modules. Five are bit-for-bit identical closures binding cwd=str(repo): evolve_reap.py:49/64, create_pr.py:41/67, forge_bitbucket.py:38/43, evolve_select.py:44/64, forge_github.py:32/35. Two differ under the SAME repeated Runner type name: evolve_self_merge.py:79-83 binds NO cwd (runs ambient); flow_beads_create.py:56/127 takes cwd per-call and is in fact a verbatim duplicate of the EXISTING Contract A default_runner body. The smell is one repeated type alias bound to three different runtime cwd behaviors across sibling evolve-cluster modules — a correctness-adjacent footgun, not LOC cosmetics.

SCOPE (from skeptic): the genuinely-uncovered shape is the 5 cwd-bound modules — add a third factory to _runner.py for them. flow_beads_create.py should simply import the existing Contract A default_runner rather than get the new factory (its body is identical). evolve_self_merge.py stays distinct (ambient, no cwd bound) and must NOT be folded into a cwd-binding factory.

BLAST RADIUS: Add a third factory to _runner.py and rewire 5 importers across forge adapters (forge_github, forge_bitbucket) + evolve cluster (evolve_reap, evolve_select) + create_pr (a WIRED handler); separately repoint flow_beads_create at Contract A. NOT mechanically behavior-preserving: a naive single-factory replacement would silently bind a cwd into evolve_self_merge it currently does not have, so the no-cwd and per-call variants must be preserved distinctly. Touches a wired handler (create_pr.py) and forge adapters.

CONFIDENCE: 0.6

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build

title: Canonical snapshot drift guard omits flow's own engine code; protects only external skill: handler bundles
description:
EVIDENCE: snapshot.py:8 docstring asserts the snapshot defends against 'a plugin reinstall could swap a handler's code', but _handlers_component (snapshot.py:134-135) iterates ONLY handlers whose value startswith 'skill:'. The flow self-target's .flow/workspace.toml [pipeline.handlers] has zero skill: entries (all inline / subagent:Plan / subagent:general-purpose / none), so the 'handlers' component is an empty dict. compute_snapshot (snapshot.py:173-178) therefore hashes only workspace_toml text + stage_registry text. known_marketplaces.json shows installLocation = the live checkout /Users/victordsm/repos/personal/flow (source=directory), and CLAUDE.md documents advancing that checkout + 'claude plugin marketplace update' mid-stream -- so advancing the checkout swaps dispatch_stage.py / state.py / diff_extract.py / inline reference docs under a running pipeline with no drift detection. dispatch_stage resolves skill_root via _skill_root_from_script() (from __file__) = the installed plugin = main checkout, same place stage_registry already reads from.

VISION ANCHOR: robustness floor names 'the canonical-snapshot drift guard (the harness config can't change mid-run undetected)'. The guard's coverage is asymmetric against its OWN stated threat model (docstring names handler-code-swap): it protects external bundles but not flow's own engine -- the code that actually runs in the maintainer self-target where mid-run checkout advances are a documented workflow.

BLAST RADIUS: snapshot.py compute path + dispatch_stage gate. Adding a tree_hash over skill_root would read from MAIN (same as stage_registry today), while reflect self-heals commit to the WORKTREE and validate next-run -- so legitimate in-run self-edits would NOT trip it, making the fix cleaner than first feared. Counter-rationale for triage: external skill bundles are independently updatable mid-run via their own marketplace, whereas flow's own engine can be defensibly treated as the trusted base for a session -- a reviewer could call current scope intended. Not a dup of flow-ghd (interrupted-sha-write fail-open, a different mechanism).

CONFIDENCE: 0.4

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (real asymmetry in named safety machinery, but engine-as-trusted-base may be intended scope; surface for maintainer triage)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: genuine maintainer-triage decision remains open (planner self-rated 78%, raised a clarifying question).

OPEN DECISION: is the engine's exclusion from the canonical-snapshot drift guard a BUG to fix, or INTENDED scope (engine-as-trusted-base for a session)? If guarded, accept the recommended BRANCH-GATED design — hash skill_root's engine tree only when skill_root is on a protected branch (main/master/dev/develop), so the guard is active exactly in the marketplace-tracks-main setup where mid-run 'git pull' + 'claude plugin marketplace update' is the real threat AND machinery_edit already REFUSES self-edits — over (a) wontfix or (b) unconditional hashing (which also needs a reload-snapshot after every machinery self-edit, expanding blast radius beyond this ticket)?

CORRECTION TO TICKET RATIONALE (verified against code): the ticket claims the fix is safe because 'reflect self-heals commit to the WORKTREE.' That is FALSE. machinery_edit.apply_edit edits skill_root's OWN working tree (not the run's worktree), only when skill_root is on a feature branch. The real reason a skill_root hash is safe in the documented (main-tracking) setup is the protected-branch REFUSAL in machinery_edit — the same _PROTECTED gate. Ticket conclusion (safe) holds; its mechanism is wrong. Branch-gating makes the guard's active window the exact complement of the self-edit-allowed window.

SHAPE IF APPROVED: add an 'engine' component (_tree_hash over skill_root, branch-gated, excluding stage-registry.toml to avoid double-coverage) to compute_snapshot/_payload/drifted_components in snapshot.py; flows through dispatch's existing classify_drift->verify->drifted_components seam with no dispatch_stage change. Confirm you want mid-run engine advances to ABORT in-flight runs (exit 1) rather than silently swap engine code.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: FIX, branch-gated. Hash skill_root engine tree into the canonical snapshot ONLY when skill_root is on a protected branch (main, master, dev, develop), active exactly in the marketplace-tracks-main threat window. Do NOT hash unconditionally (avoids a reload-snapshot after every machinery self-edit).

title: heartbeat producer side is unwired -- hung detection is structurally blind
description:
EVIDENCE: heartbeat.py:102 write_progress + the 'write' CLI subcommand (heartbeat.py:267,297) have zero callers outside heartbeat.py's own cli_main and tests -- verified via grep over scripts/*.py (non-test) and SKILL.md+references/*.md. quarantine_stale (167) + identity_ok (144) are reachable only from write_progress, so transitively dead in production too. The consumer recover.py:69-71 calls only read_progress + detect_hung, which therefore always read None / never classify hung. inventory.md:833 records enforcement was deferred to 'phase 7-full ships heartbeat', which never wired the producer. (MODULE.md:15 'imported by recover/state/_registry' is misleading: state.py only name-drops in comments; _registry.py only parses default_heartbeat_required, a field stage-registry.toml:13 itself flags INFORMATIONAL ONLY.)

VISION ANCHOR: 'A mismatch is ground truth; the repair is not' (VISION:44) -- consumer wired, producer not, is a half-built feature: recover detect emits a 'progress' hung-classification map that is structurally always empty.

BLAST RADIUS: heartbeat.py producer functions (write_progress/quarantine_stale/identity_ok) + the 'write' CLI subcommand + their tests. recover.py keeps read_progress/detect_hung (consumer path untouched). Either deletion (remove producer + downgrade recover detect hung-classification to the no-op it already effectively is) or wiring (call 'heartbeat write' from the do-loop per-op so detection fires) -- both change recover's effective behavior, so NOT behavior-preserving.

CONFIDENCE: 0.82

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (delete the dead producer vs wire it into the do-loop is a real direction call; both shift recover's effective behavior)

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve: this is a genuine direction call a human must settle. DELETE the dead heartbeat producer vs WIRE it into the pipeline.

Planner findings (both directions change recover detect's effective behavior, so neither is behavior-preserving):
- Producer (write_progress / 'heartbeat write' CLI / quarantine_stale / identity_ok) has ZERO non-test callers; consumer recover.py:69-71 therefore always reads None and emits an always-empty 'progress' hung-map.
- WIRE is NOT the 'call heartbeat write from the do-loop per-op' one-liner the ticket implies: the do-loop is stage-granular (no per-op seam), so wiring means editing every long-running references/stage-*.md to emit per-op writes, adding a quarantine_stale stage-start call site, AND new consumer prose in verb-recover.md to actually act on the hung classification (it ignores the map today). skill: handlers are opaque and cannot cooperate.
- DELETE is minimal and in-scope: feature was deferred to 'phase 7-full ships heartbeat' (inventory.md:833) and never wired; VISION:44 ('a mismatch is ground truth; the repair is not') argues against a detection that can never fire. Planner recommends DELETE.

OPEN QUESTION: DELETE (recommended) or WIRE? To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: DELETE the dead heartbeat producer. Remove write_progress, the heartbeat write CLI, quarantine_stale and identity_ok (zero non-test callers) plus the always-empty recover.py:69-71 consumer that reads them. Do NOT wire it into the pipeline.

title: Wired stage error-prose points to 5 /flow recover remediation flags that don't exist
description:
EVIDENCE: references/stage-ticket.md:100 (/flow recover --reset-ticket <KEY>), :102 (--reset-frontmatter); references/stage-commit.md:45 & :57 (/flow recover --reset-baseline), :88 (--reapply-implement '(phase 8c)'). recover.py --help exposes ONLY {detect,takeover,abort,reload-snapshot,retry,skip} -- none of these 5 flags exist (verified via python3 recover.py --help). verb-recover.md (the canonical recover ref) and recover.py agree on the real surface; only these two wired ## Errors sections name the phantom flags. seam_check.py cannot catch it: it lints ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts/*.py invocations, not /flow recover --x prose.

VISION ANCHOR: 'the code is what's true now' (VISION:44) + robustness floor. recover verb has SHIPPED (recover.py + verb-recover.md, 6 subcommands) with a DIFFERENT surface than the '(phase 8c)' breadcrumbs promised, so these are stale-and-wrong, not honest placeholders: the doc advertises --reset-*/--reapply-implement flags the shipped impl never delivered. title: Commit content-ownership gate is filename-level only; unplanned hunks in an allowed file slip through
description:
Content-ownership commit gate verifies touched FILES against planned_files, not hunks. A planned file edited beyond the planned change passes the gate, so out-of-plan hunks ride into the PR.

EVIDENCE:
- diff_extract.py:270-313 (check_ownership) compares touched FILES against baseline planned_files only; a file in planned_files passes regardless of which/how many hunks changed.
- diff_extract.py:277-279 docstring: 'Filename-level gate ... Hunk-level ownership against implement.diff is a deeper check deferred to a later phase.'
- diff_extract.py:255 capture_implement_diff runs 'git diff --binary --raw <head_sha> -- <planned_files>' (pathspec-scoped), capturing EVERY hunk in a planned file including out-of-plan ones; stage-commit.md:84 'git apply --cached --binary implement.diff' then stages all of them.
- stage-commit.md:9 NOT 'git add .' only excludes files OUTSIDE planned_files, not stray hunks inside a planned file.
- dev-history.md:22 + inventory.md:613-619 independently flag the same deferral.
- VISION.md:70 names 'the content-ownership commit gate (a PR holds only what was planned)' as a floor property.

PROPOSED: tighten diff_extract.py from file- to hunk-granularity ownership.

BLAST RADIUS: Tightens diff_extract.py (a named safety-guard file) from file- to hunk-granularity; affects every commit's ownership check. Risk: hunk-attribution false-positives blocking legit edits. Robustness-adjacent. Does NOT pre-authorize widening any gate (VISION:74) -- maintainer decision.

CONFIDENCE: 0.4

RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion
close_reason: Not implementable as specified: a hunk-level ownership gate would compare implement.diff to itself (tautology, always passes). No intended-hunks ground-truth artifact exists. Closing; revisit only as a from-scratch intended-hunks artifact design.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text:
flow --auto could not self-approve (planner returned clarifying questions; matches the ticket own confidence 0.4 / needs-discussion).

FINDING: the proposed hunk-level ownership gate is NOT implementable as stated. A hunk gate needs a ground-truth record of WHICH hunks were authorized. None exists: the plan stage emits planned_files (file paths only); baseline.json stores planned_files + pre-implement blob shas (the BEFORE state); implement.diff is by construction the implement stage entire delta over the planned files. So the only thing finer than file-level the gate could compare against is implement.diff itself, and a hunk gate comparing implement.diff to implement.diff is a tautology that always passes. The set it would enforce is exactly the set that already leaks. Tightening therefore requires a NEW input (an intended-hunks artifact), which is a new plan-stage output + baseline-schema change + gate change = a stage-contract change on the what-flow-is-for side of the ownership line (VISION:74), a maintainer decision.

OPEN QUESTIONS:
1. Is there an intended-hunks / line-range ground-truth source anywhere in flow that the planner missed? If so, point to it and the real gate can be planned against it.
2. If none, which landing: (A) close as not-implementable-as-stated and correct the misleading deferred/later-phase docs (diff_extract.py:277-279, inventory.md:615-620; VISION:70 flagged for maintainer edit) so the codebase stops implying it is merely queued work; (B) open a new DESIGN ticket to introduce an intended-hunks artifact; or (C) leave as-is, needs-discussion.

To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

title: [proposal] make the auto-vs-propose line a backlog split, not a label filter
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (structural change to the evolve subsystem — judgment; runs via /flow <key> through the plan gate, NOT drained). Cleaner: make the line a BACKLOG SPLIT. drain only ever queries 'bd ready -l evolve' (evolve_select.py:17,223) and merge='inline' is repo-level (workspace.toml:53, self-merges any run), so a PLAIN (non-evolve) bead is simply invisible to drain and runs through the normal /flow <key> pipeline — where the spec front-half IS the triage and ExitPlanMode IS the accept. Engaging a proposal is already the flow; it needs zero proposal-specific machinery.

CHANGES:
1. evolve propose (verb-evolve.md §propose step C): file JUDGMENT findings as PLAIN 'proposal' beads (label 'proposal' only, NOT 'evolve'). Provably-safe findings stay in the autonomous lane (labelled 'evolve').
2. DELETE held_proposal from evolve_select.py (lines ~129-143,155,190): once propose stops dual-labelling, no evolve bead ever carries 'proposal', so the filter is dead. Drop it + its tests.
3. DROP the 'audit' label as a required tag (it is decorative — nothing in evolve_select branches on it; the lane is defined by 'evolve', identity by the auto-added evid:/evidfile: fingerprints). evolve propose/audit provably-safe findings get just 'evolve' (+ 'hot' when guard-touching). Update §audit step 3 + §propose step C label guidance.
4. drain Report (verb-evolve.md §drain): remove the held_proposal section (nothing is held in the evolve lane anymore).
5. self-evolution.md: reframe the auto-vs-propose line as the backlog split — 'evolve'-labelled = the loop's autonomous lane (drain); plain 'proposal' = the maintainer's lane (/flow <key>, plan gate). Update the diagram/Consumer text.
6. MIGRATION: the existing proposal beads (flow-eia, flow-g3e, flow-837, flow-0mi, flow-148, flow-3ji) get 'evolve' stripped (keep 'proposal') — done out-of-band when this was agreed.

LANE MODEL (the new mental model):
  evolve-labelled  -> autonomous lane  -> drain --auto, unattended   (audit findings + reflect-sling friction)
  plain 'proposal' -> human-driven lane -> you: /flow <key>, plan gate (generative judgment work)

ACCEPTANCE: evolve_select has no held_proposal; an evolve propose run files a judgment finding as a non-evolve 'proposal' bead; bd ready -l evolve never shows a proposal; seam_check + lint + test green; verb-evolve/self-evolution describe the backlog split.

RELATED: companion to flow-5a6 (/flow triage for the DEFERRED queue) — together they define the two held/parked queues: deferred (run started, parked on the plan gate -> /flow triage) and proposal (never started, awaits the plan gate -> /flow <key>). Neither needs a relabel hack.

title: [proposal] machinery_edit protected-branch refusal is bypassed in detached-HEAD state
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (defensive hardening of a keystone guard — judgment on the refusal predicate's default). CONFIDENCE 0.4 (LOW — mechanically real but skeptic showed it is not reachable today). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (one-line + one test, cheap insurance). HOT — touches machinery_edit.py, a named guard file.

MECHANICAL FACT (verified empirically): _current_branch (machinery_edit.py:66-85) runs 'git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD', which returns the literal 'HEAD' in detached-HEAD state; _PROTECTED={'main','master','dev','develop'} (line 63) does not contain 'HEAD', so 'branch in _PROTECTED' (line 126) is False and the apply PROCEEDS. The protected-branch refusal is silently bypassed when detached.

WHY LOW CONFIDENCE (honest, from an adversarial skeptic that REFUTED the original 'hot reachable hole' framing): the guard resolves SKILL-ROOT's own working tree (the marketplace plugin checkout), which no flow path detaches — it sits on main (marketplace-tracks-main) or a feature branch (maintainer dogfooding). The CLAUDE.md:43 detached-HEAD note is about a throwaway gh-pr-merge spot, a DIFFERENT working tree. And a detached commit does not advance main's ref, so it is outside the guard's stated main-ref-advance threat model. So this is a silent-failure HOLE on a keystone guard, but not currently reachable.

WHY FILE ANYWAY (not dropped): it is a one-line predicate fix (treat 'HEAD'/empty/detached as refuse-by-default) + one test, on a safety guard, where green will never catch the gap. Cheap insurance against a future path that does detach skill-root. Maintainer's call whether the defense is worth the line.

Blast radius: machinery_edit.py only; predicate change + one test. No caller changes. Rides the hot path (guard file).

VERIFIED: architecture-coherence lens + independent skeptic (skeptic refuted reachability, confirmed the mechanical fact — filed as low-value defensive proposal, NOT audit, NOT a current hot hole).

title: [proposal] revert-rate (3rd vision-named measure) has neither a metric nor a capture seam
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (feature + a design question — judgment). CONFIDENCE 0.5. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: needs-discussion (scope the capture mechanism before building). NOT hot.

Evidence: VISION.md:54 names 'a lower revert rate' alongside time-to-PR and friction-per-run as what recall --metric is the seam for. No revert/regression signal is captured anywhere: grep revert|rollback|reopen across scripts hits only compose_commit.py:30 (commit-type enum), tracker_beads.py:500 (bd reopen transition primitive, not joined to ship-events), and beads status strings. observe_ship_event.py writes ship-events that are IMMUTABLE once created and record only forward shipping (shipped_at + evidence) — by construction they cannot carry a post-ship revert. So unlike friction-per-run, this has NO feedstock to read yet.

SCOPING QUESTION (for triage): what counts as a revert — a git revert commit, a reopened bead, a reverted/closed PR? The answer decides the capture mechanism (a post-merge revert observer vs a tracker-reopen-to-ship-event join). 2-part: observe, THEN measure.

Rationale: without a revert measure, 'auto-ship only on ground truth' has no way to detect that an auto-shipped change later proved wrong — the exact failure the ground-truth gate bounds.

VERIFIED: feature lens + independent skeptic SURVIVES (gap is real; docked to 0.5 — naming-in-vision is not felt friction, and the capture design is unscoped).

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: flow --auto could not self-approve: this is a proposal with an unresolved scoping question that must be settled before any code is written. BLOCKING: what counts as a revert, and therefore which capture mechanism to build — (A) a reopened/re-closed bead joined to its ship-event at metric-compute time [recommended: only option with an existing trigger point, reuses the classify_attribution join pattern, but reopen is a noisy proxy]; (B) a git revert commit observer [highest-fidelity, but no trigger point in a pipeline that ends at draft-PR]; or (C) a reverted/closed-PR observer [forge-coupled, no post-merge trigger point]? This answer fixes the revert-count numerator schema and the entire observe-half file list. The measure half (a revert-rate subcommand in metric.py reusing load_ship_events as the denominator + verb-recall.md docs) is specifiable now; the observe half is blocked on this. Note the hard invariant: ship-events are immutable by O_EXCL construction, so revert capture must be additive (new parallel record or pure compute-time join), never a new field on the ship-event. To unstick: answer here, reopen (status->open), and re-run WITHOUT --auto to plan interactively.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: DECISION: Reopened-bead join (option A). Define a revert as a shipped bead that is reopened and re-closed, joined to its ship-event at metric-compute time (reuse the classify_attribution join pattern). Accept reopen as a noisy proxy. Measure half: a revert-rate subcommand in metric.py reusing load_ship_events as the denominator, plus verb-recall.md docs.

title: [proposal] recall --metric can't report friction-events-per-run (feedstock captured but unread)
description:
DISPOSITION: proposal (new measurement capability — judgment about what to count / how to window). CONFIDENCE 0.72. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build (low-risk, read-only, closes a vision-named loop). NOT hot.

Blast radius: a new friction-per-run subparser in metric.py + a forwarding line in recall.py (--metric passthrough already exists). Adds a reader, no writer, no schema change. Read-only over existing JSONL.

title: Add /flow triage: surface + reopen deferred tickets (no mechanism today)
description:
GAP: there is NO mechanism to tackle deferred tickets. An --auto run that cannot self-approve (clarifying question, or sub-90% with a reachable gap) sets status->deferred + comments its open questions, then exits (verb-spec.md auto path). Deferred is UNIVERSAL, not maintainer-only (verb-spec.md: 'behavior is universal'); any user firing /flow <ticket> --auto in parallel (the primary fire-and-forget path) accumulates deferred tickets. But deferred beads go INVISIBLE: they drop out of bd ready (memory bd-ready-excludes-deferred); evolve_select.py:49 treats a CLOSED-or-DEFERRED bead as never-in-flight so drain never launches/parks them; drain's 'parked' only catches in-flight-with-dead-lease (evolve_drain.py:63), NOT historically-deferred. So drain's Report says 'review your deferred beads' (verb-evolve.md:150) but never lists WHICH or WHAT they asked. Recovery today is a fully manual recipe: read the comment, bd update --status open, re-run interactively.

GOAL: a top-level work-state verb /flow triage that surfaces the deferred queue with each bead's open-question comment inline, and reopens one with an answer in a single step. The DECISION stays human (re-run is manual + interactive); the verb automates SURFACING + the reopen mechanics only. Do NOT auto-retry --auto (it would re-defer on the same question).

PLACEMENT: top-level verb, same family as status/recover/sync/baseline (all top-level, none maintainer-gated). NOT the evolve namespace (its Gate refuses non-maintainer workspaces -> would lock users out of unsticking their own runs).

SHAPE:
  /flow triage                    -> list deferred beads + each one's last 'could not self-approve' comment inline.
  /flow triage <key> "answer..."  -> post the answer as a comment + transition deferred->open; print the re-run-without---auto hint.

TRACKER-AGNOSTIC (go through the tracker_cli seam, not raw bd):
  - read open-question: tracker_cli get already returns 'comments' (confirmed).
  - reopen deferred->open: tracker_beads.py:78 allows 'deferred': ['open','closed'] -> reachable via tracker_cli transition. (Only transition TO deferred is blocked per memory tracker-cli-cannot-transition-beads-to-deferred; reopen FROM deferred is fine.)
  - list deferred: SEAM GAP. tracker_cli has get/list-assigned/state/transition/comment/create/is-shipped/download-attachments but NO status-filter list. Add 'tracker_cli list --status <status>' delegating to the tracker adapter (beads: bd list --status deferred --json, title key per memory bd-list-json-uses-title-key). Wire it into the adapter protocol + a beads impl; jira adapter can raise NotImplemented for now if needed.

FILES (anticipated): NEW scripts/triage.py (+ tests/test_triage.py); tracker_cli.py + the tracker adapter protocol + tracker_beads.py (new list-by-status seam, + tests); references/verb-triage.md (new); SKILL.md router table line + verb listing; MODULE.md + inventory.md entries; verb-evolve.md drain Report pointer to /flow triage; version bump plugin.json + marketplace.json in sync; run seam_check.py (new prose->CLI invocations).

ACCEPTANCE: /flow triage lists flow-wo5 (currently deferred) with its commented open question; /flow triage flow-wo5 "<answer>" comments + flips it to open; seam_check green; lint+test green.

HOT: touches SKILL.md (router) + tracker seam -> rides hot review.

title: merge stage push-state guard trips on untracked .flow/tickets/, defeating Layer-2 self-merge
description: The merge stage (references/stage-merge.md:52) gates self-merge on '[ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]' to bind the merge to the CI'd SHA. But .gitignore:28 ignores only .flow/runs/, NOT .flow/tickets/. flow_worktree.py create writes .flow/tickets/<KEY>.md every run, so git status --porcelain is ALWAYS non-empty ('?? .flow/tickets/') after bootstrap. The guard therefore trips on flow's own untracked scratch and defers EVERY non-hot eligible self-merge to the janitor, even when HEAD==@{u} and the tracked tree is clean (the actual PR diff is fully committed+pushed+CI'd). Observed on flow-2fe (PR #90): gate returned action:merge, CI green, but §3 porcelain check forced a skip. Fix options: (a) add '.flow/tickets/' to .gitignore alongside '.flow/runs/' (root cause — tickets/ is per-run transient state like runs/); and/or (b) tighten the stage-merge.md guard to ignore untracked flow scaffolding (e.g. check 'git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no' plus an unpushed-commit check, since the real concern is tracked/committed-but-un-CI'd code, not untracked flow scratch). Evidence: .gitignore:27-38, references/stage-merge.md:46-63, git ls-files .flow (only .initialized + workspace.toml tracked). Friction id 423810c5696f47d180e972f11683adda.
close_reason: Moot: superseded by #91 — the merge push-state guard now uses 'git status --porcelain --untracked-files=no', so untracked .flow/tickets/ scratch no longer trips it. Validated live (flow-xdj self-merged #92).

title: flow_worktree.py reap_worktree docstring frames leak via dropped --delete-branch
description: flow_worktree.py:231 (reap_worktree docstring) explains the local-branch leak by reference to 'gh pr merge --delete-branch removes only the remote branch'. title: content-ownership gate false-flags planned files whose path contains a space
description: diff_extract.check_ownership parses git status --porcelain paths with line[3:].strip() (diff_extract.py:299). Porcelain v1 double-quotes any path containing a space (e.g. ?? "a b.py") regardless of core.quotePath. The literal quotes survive into changed, so a planned file whose path has a space never matches the owned set (which holds the unquoted a b.py), lands in unowned_changes -> ok=False -> exit 3, and the commit gate blocks a legitimate commit forever. Scope: isolated to check_ownership; capture_implement_diff/_untracked_files use git ls-files which does not quote. Fix: unquote porcelain paths (strip surrounding quotes + decode C-escapes) before matching. Evidence: diff_extract.py:294,299. Blast radius: small, only paths with spaces; pure parse fix in one function. HOT: guard file diff_extract.py.

title: evolve --ship §6A merges before reaping worktree: gh exits 1, leaks remote branch, breaks bd-close chain
description: REPRODUCED this --ship pass. references/verb-evolve.md §6A (lines 76-85) runs 'gh pr merge <pr> --squash --delete-branch && bd close <key>' BEFORE flow_worktree.py reap. Because the worktree still holds the feature branch, gh's local-branch delete fails ('cannot delete branch ... used by worktree'), so gh EXITS 1 -> the && short-circuits and 'bd close' never runs (had to close 6 beads by hand). The remote branch is ALSO left undeleted: after the exit-1 merge, all 6 just-merged branches plus 7 older ones (13 total) were still present on origin via git ls-remote. Stale remote feature/flow-* refs feed evolve_select.py:_hot_inflight (line 215-229), which counted closed hot beads aut.6/bly as in-flight and held ALL 4 ready hot beads (launch=[] until I manually 'git push origin --delete' 13 branches). flow-26g (just merged) patched the closed-bead side in the selector but not the branch leak itself. FIX: in §6A, run flow_worktree.py reap (which frees+deletes the local branch) BEFORE gh pr merge, OR drop --delete-branch and let reap own all branch deletion incl. remote; either way decouple 'bd close' from gh's exit code. VALUE: makes the drainer's reap self-cleaning instead of leaking refs that silently block future hot launches. BLAST RADIUS: prose recipe in references/verb-evolve.md (hot: §6A drainer path).

title: evolve drainer: _hot_inflight counts CLOSED/deferred hot beads as in-flight via leaked branches
description:
GROUND TRUTH: evolve_select._hot_inflight (scripts/evolve_select.py:215-230) spuriously holds the only ready hot bead because it treats CLOSED hot beads as occupying the single hot slot.

ROOT CAUSE: _hot_inflight queries hot beads with _EVOLVE_STATUSES (scripts/evolve_select.py:48 = 'open,in_progress,blocked,deferred,closed'). A CLOSED hot bead whose squash-merged remote branch was never deleted (reap --delete-branch postdates older PRs) still has a 'feature/<key>-*' ref in _gather_refs' all-branches scan. inflight_flow_keys & hot_keys is then non-empty -> hot_inflight=True -> partition holds the next ready hot bead under held_hot forever.

REPRO (observed this pass): open_pr_count=0, nothing hot actually running, yet select() returned launch=[], held_hot=['flow-ghd']. Stale remote branches feature/flow-kos-* and feature/flow-a0t-* map to closed hot beads flow-kos+flow-a0t -> false positive.

FIX (tighten, minimal): _hot_inflight should query only TRULY-active statuses 'open,in_progress,blocked' (a CLOSED or DEFERRED bead is never in flight regardless of a leaked branch). Do NOT reuse _EVOLVE_STATUSES there (that constant is correct for the closed-inclusive dedup path _is_inflight uses). Introduce a separate active-only status set for the hot-slot query. Blast radius is one function: _EVOLVE_STATUSES is referenced ONLY by _hot_inflight (line 221), so narrowing a NEW constant leaves the dedup path untouched. Do NOT rearchitect the all-branches scan (intentional: catches a pre-PR bg run). Add a unit test: a closed hot bead + its leaked ref must NOT set hot_inflight.

EVIDENCE: scripts/evolve_select.py:48 (_EVOLVE_STATUSES), :215-230 (_hot_inflight), :192-211 (_gather_refs all-branches scan).
BLAST RADIUS: scripts/evolve_select.py. ~3-line change in one function + one test. No API change. NON-HOT (evolve_select.py is drainer machinery, not a guard file).

title: SKILL.md frames the deliverable as a draft PR, but create_pr opens ready-for-review by default -- which contract is right?
description:
JUDGMENT / user-facing contract (held for maintainer triage, NOT auto-drainable). A mismatch is ground truth; the repair direction here is NOT -- it changes what the user experiences as flow's deliverable, which the vision reserves for the maintainer.

THE MISMATCH (evidence). SKILL.md repeatedly frames the deliverable as a *draft* PR and asserts draft is the normal end state: SKILL.md:3,4,15,21,22 ('draft PR') and SKILL.md:189-191 ('Draft state is the normal end state, not a caveat: never flag it'). But create_pr.py opens **ready-for-review** unless [create_pr] draft=true: create_pr.py:6-7,44-53 (_draft_config default False),113,170. The sibling doc references/stage-create_pr.md:3,12 and verb-do.md:7 also describe ready-for-review. So SKILL.md contradicts both the code and its own sibling docs.

WHY THIS IS A PROPOSAL, NOT AN AUTO-FIX. The draft-vs-ready PR is the user-facing output contract ('you review the draft PR'). Aligning it either way changes the experience, so it is 'what flow is for' = maintainer's call. The --auto defer-valve would NOT catch this: the evidence makes 'align docs to ready' look unambiguous, so a headless planner clears 90% confidently and proceeds; and a prose-only change does not trip the section-6A property review (which checks lease/snapshot/ownership/flock, not contract wording). So left as audit it would auto-merge a deliverable-contract change with no maintainer eyes.

THE FORK:
  A) SKILL.md is stale -> update its prose to 'ready-for-review' (and stop calling draft the normal end state).
  B) create_pr.py's default is the bug -> the whole product framing is draft-as-deliverable with plan-approval as the one human gate, so the code default should flip to draft=true.
Note: the dogfood .flow/workspace.toml:29 sets draft=false, but that is THIS repo's config, not evidence about the general default for deployed user projects.

CONFIDENCE: 0.55 that A is intended (code + sibling docs already say ready, and this repo runs ready). RECOMMENDED DEFAULT: build A (align SKILL.md prose to ready-for-review) -- but confirm, because B reframes the headline gate story. BLAST: SKILL.md is hot (loaded every run); whichever way, the fix is small and prose/config-only. HOT: touches SKILL.md.

comments.author: Victor De Simone
comments.text: evolve cold-audit (doc-drift miner): the same draft-vs-ready root surfaces in stage-registry.toml:121 too. The create_pr handler description claims 'push + gh pr create --draft --fill, idempotent', but create_pr.py defaults to ready-for-review (draft only under [create_pr] draft=true / --draft) and explicitly avoids --fill (create_pr.py:113,127). When flow-rvc resolves the draft-vs-ready direction, fix stage-registry.toml:121 in the same change (which way it reads depends on the resolution). The --fill half is direction-independent: the description should drop --fill regardless. Not filed as a separate audit bead to avoid prejudging this held decision.

title: sync.py reconcile exception-resilience branch untested: a throwing tracker call could silently drop a queued mutation

title: MODULE.md mis-describes create_pr.py as 'gh pr create --draft --fill' and omits its --draft flag
description: DOC DRIFT (doc->code). MODULE.md:60 documents create_pr.py behavior as 'push branch + gh pr create --draft --fill' with flags '--workspace-root [--base main --ticket KEY]'. Code reality: create_pr.py:127-128 explicitly does NOT use --fill (comment 'Not gh --fill: ...'); create_pr.py:144-145 appends --draft ONLY when draft config is true (default ready-for-review, create_pr.py:6-7,44-53,170); and create_pr.py:163-166 exposes a --draft flag that MODULE.md omits. (Related but distinct from the SKILL.md draft-default proposal -- this is a pure map-vs-code fix, the establishment that create_pr does not use --fill is independent.)

title: seam_check.scripts_missing_from_module_md enforced only in tests, never wired into the CLI gate
description: GATE DIVERGENCE (additive, behavior-preserving). seam_check.scripts_missing_from_module_md (seam_check.py:282) implements the MODULE.md-completeness invariant, but the CLI entrypoint main() (seam_check.py:305) never calls it -- its only callers are in tests/test_seam_check.py:109,114,122,131 (109 is the live test_live_docs_are_green assertion). So 'python3 seam_check.py' (the CLI that CI and the prose seam invoke) and the pytest suite check DIFFERENT invariants: a maintainer running the CLI by hand will not catch a script missing from MODULE.md. VALUE: makes the CLI gate and its test suite enforce the same contract; the check already runs green in tests so wiring it into main() will not newly fail CI. RESOLUTION: call scripts_missing_from_module_md from main() and surface it as an error (do NOT delete it). BLAST: low -- adds an already-green check to the CLI surface.
